title: Mouse pointer does not scale when remote = 1080p, client = 4K (200% DPI)
author: HubKing
body_snippet:
My client monitor is 4K. I use 200% DPI. If I set "Fullscreen" like below, the server's screen becomes 1080p. That is fine because 4K would be slower, but the problem is that the mouse pointer remains unscaled.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10900489/167331538-7d832cf7-2337-4b02-9f6c-087e6c49c5a9.png)

PS: If I disable "Automatic Resize", which makes the remote resolution 4K, I think, the cursor is still the same as when "Automatic Size" is enabled.

## Expected Behavi...

iterations.description: The mouse pointer in RDP sessions does not scale correctly on high-DPI client machines, leading to a poor user experience.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The mouse pointer in RDP sessions does not scale correctly on high-DPI client machines, leading to a poor user experience.
Approach: Investigate how RDP DPI settings are passed to the MSTSCLib control, specifically looking for properties or methods related to display scaling or smart sizing that might affect mouse pointer rendering.
